name: Switcher Layout Intent
summary: A primitive layout region-role (#1680 taxonomy): composition-intent = flip horizontal↔vertical at a content-width threshold (container-driven, no media query). Identity is the arrangement, not the CSS — FUI ships one block per role and the mechanism (flex-wrap + a min() basis trick (or container queries)) is an impl seam. Project-less; per-project styling rides presentational variants + plateau assembler presets.

threshold.description: The container inline-size at which the layout flips from a horizontal row to a vertical stack — the role's essential knob (container-driven, no viewport breakpoint).

gap.description: Even spacing between items in either orientation, on a spacing scale.

limit.description: The maximum number of items allowed in the horizontal row before it is forced to the vertical layout regardless of width. Absent → no item limit.

description:
What This Is

Flips between a horizontal row and a vertical stack based on whether the container is wider than a threshold — not a viewport breakpoint, a true container-driven switch. Adaptive toolbars, responsive form rows.

Composition-intent (the identity)

A primitive layout region-role (layoutTier: region-role), one of the per-role taxonomy ratified in #1680. Its identity is the composition-intent — flip horizontal↔vertical at a content-width threshold (container-driven, no media query) — impl-agnostic. FUI ships exactly one block for this role; the CSS mechanism (flex-wrap + a min() basis trick (or container queries)) is an impl seam, never the role's identity (platform-decisions.md#layout-role-composition-intent).

Dimensions (UX-only)

  • threshold: The container inline-size at which the layout flips from a horizontal row to a vertical stack — the role's essential knob (container-driven, no viewport breakpoint).
  • gap: Even spacing between items in either orientation, on a spacing scale (none · xs · sm · md · lg · xl).
  • limit: The maximum number of items allowed in the horizontal row before it is forced to the vertical layout regardless of width. Absent → no item limit.

Annotation, not identity

Distinct from stack / cluster — it auto-switches axis at a threshold rather than fixing one. The earns-role test is met.
